title: Phase5 custom-primary gate: sha256 not enforced at runtime + generic phase5_custom module-name collision
source: hackathon-pm B0040 materialization demo 2026-05-29 (XAGUSD BULL_QUIET phase5_custom).
why: Two doc/code drifts found while demonstrating the materialization->audit path: (1) custom_primary_sha256 is documented as enforced by the dispatcher (phase5-regime-methodology SKILL.md ~line 211), but scripts/run_xau_d1.py and phase5/run_proposal.py never read/verify it (grep sha256 -> 0 matches) -- the materialization gate is existence-only. So custom code can drift from its attested hash undetected. (2) _select_primary imports pipeline.primaries_phase5.<p.primary>; a proposal whose primary is the literal string phase5_custom materializes to phase5_custom.py -- so multiple distinct phase5_custom proposals collide on one module path. (Materialized survivors that set primary=phase5_<id> avoid this.)
value_rationale: Medium: trust/reproducibility gap (sha256) + a real collision footgun if >1 phase5_custom proposal is materialized. Not blocking the established edge-null conclusion.
scope: Either wire a sha256 check into the dispatcher (reject if module hash != custom_primary_sha256) OR correct SKILL.md to state the hash is a commit-time attestation only.
Require materialized customs to set primary=phase5_<unique_id> (and have the orchestrator rewrite phase5_custom -> phase5_<id> at materialization) so module paths never collide.

history.reason: feat(loop-a): B0040 custom-primary materialization->audit path demonstrated; B0085 normalizer; B0086 logged
